'''''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 14''', also labeled as '''"The Warrior Princess, Chapter 2"''', is the fourteenth issue in the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' series of comics. It was released on December 26, 1996 by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''When the royal court of Eiattu 6 came to Rogue Squadron to collect their princess—Plourr Ilo, who was unaware of her heritage—they did so to help put an end to their planet's internal rebellion. They reasoned that a strong figurehead might pull the people together—what they couldn't know was that a strong-willed princess might have her own view of how things should be run.''

==Plot summary==
Members of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] patrol the swamps of [[Eiattu 6]] on the backs of [[thuvasaur]]s. [[Dllr Nep]] hears [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire nearby as Wedge Antilles investigates the site of a previous [[People's Liberation Battalion]] (PLB) attack on an Imperial base. Despite [[Isplourrdacartha Estillo|Plourr's]] protests, Wedge allows her legal husband and second cousin [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Rial Pernon]] to fly with them to give the other Rogues air support. Before the Rogues become overwhelmed with troopers and TIEs, a group of PLB troops arrives led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Ashaad]], who embraces the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] as fellow enemies of the Empire. At base, PLB leader Count [[Arian Laabann]] of the [[Priamsta]] invites Plourr and her compatriots to dinner. 

That night, [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] and the other grounded Rogues attend a fiery speech held by [[Harrandatha Estillo]], Plourr's long-lost brother and leader of the PLB, who asks Janson about his sister. At the Primsta dinner, Plourr renounces the nobles and refuses them a place in Eiattu's future, likening them to [[Poison/Legends|poison]]. 

Elsewhere, a captured PLB fighter is tortured by an [[Leonia Tavira|Imperial officer]]. Though he resists giving up any information, he is greeted by a man who appears to be Harran. "Harran" breaks the man's neck and the officer embraces him, claiming that he will soon rule Eiattu and that nobody will be able to stop them.
